name: Dstl — UK Defence Science & Technology Lab
overview: Dstl is the Ministry of Defence's in-house science and technology agency, and the technical engine behind much of the UK's defence AI work — it sits beneath the Defence AI Centre and runs the labs, experiments and research contracts. Its public footprint is strongest on building AI/data-science capability (an 'AI Lab', regional hubs, academic research centres), on procurement frameworks like Serapis that open the supply chain to SMEs and universities, and on a flagship £7M experimentation contract with Kainos, Faculty and Actica. Fielded operational systems are mostly undisclosed; what's public is the research scaffolding, the partnerships and the contracts.

established.label: Dstl's flagship AI team
established.plain: Dstl's single front door for AI, machine learning and data science work. Rather than scattering AI expertise across the lab, the AI Lab pulls it together as a pan-Dstl flagship that partners with industry and academia to apply AI responsibly to defence problems — from intelligence analysis to autonomous platforms. It's Dstl's main contribution to the wider Defence AI Centre.
established.sourceName: GOV.UK — AI and data science: defence S&T capability

established.label: The £220-300M supplier framework
established.plain: A large, multi-year procurement framework Dstl uses to buy research across command-and-control, intelligence, surveillance, space and simulation. Its purpose is to widen the door — pulling in SMEs, non-traditional suppliers and universities, not just big primes. One of its lots (Lot 6) specifically covers autonomy, AI and machine learning, making Serapis a main channel for Dstl AI research contracts.
established.sourceName: GOV.UK — Dstl announces Serapis framework agreement

established.label: A northern AI talent base
established.plain: Dstl set up a new AI and data-science team at the National Innovation Centre for Data in Newcastle, recruiting around 15 researchers, data scientists and engineers. The aim was extra capacity, resilience and diversity in AI talent, drawing on northern universities and spreading defence AI work beyond the south.
established.sourceName: GOV.UK — AI and data science expansion for Dstl in the north

established.label: Dstl + Alan Turing Institute lab
established.plain: A joint research centre run by Dstl with the Alan Turing Institute, the UK's national AI institute. It tackles harder, longer-horizon AI questions — learning from very little data, AI for war-gaming, understanding where models fail, and ethics — and was set up to create new academic posts focused on AI for defence. Launched alongside the 2022 Defence AI Strategy.
established.sourceName: Dstl press release — Launching the Defence Centre for AI Research

established.label: Getting data ready for AI
established.plain: A centre of excellence, led by the University of Exeter with Liverpool, Surrey and Digital Catapult, set up under the Serapis framework. Its focus is the unglamorous-but-essential part of AI: getting raw defence data into a usable state, since teams can spend up to 80% of their time just preparing data. It's billed as the UK defence sector's first dedicated data-research centre.
established.sourceName: GOV.UK — Dstl launches Defence Data Research Centre

inProgress.label: Dstl's AI 'try-it-fast' engine
inProgress.plain: Dstl's flagship AI experimentation contract, worth £7M, with Kainos as agile delivery partner alongside Faculty and Actica. It funds up to 20 data-science jobs and powers Dstl's AI & Autonomous Systems Experimentation Hub — a place to rapidly prototype and test AI ideas (like helping autonomous systems understand their surroundings) before they ever reach the front line.
inProgress.sourceName: Defence Online — MoD AI strategy, Faculty and Kainos contract

inProgress.label: AI shared across the UK-US-Australia pact
inProgress.plain: Dstl-developed AI algorithms were used to process data from US maritime patrol aircraft for the first time, under the AUKUS partnership's advanced-capabilities pillar. The point is interoperability — getting the three allies' AI to work on each other's data — here to speed up anti-submarine warfare analysis. A concrete sign Dstl's AI is being trialled with allies, not just in the lab.
inProgress.sourceName: Dstl press release — Dstl AI success with AUKUS

inProgress.label: AI for overwhelmed warship crews
inProgress.plain: Run via the Defence and Security Accelerator (DASA), the Intelligent Ship project funds AI and machine learning to help future warship crews handle information overload — sorting the flood of sensor data so people can focus on decisions. It's one of Dstl's longer-running, more visible applied-AI programmes for the Royal Navy.

latest.label: Plain-English AI explainer
latest.plain: Dstl published a public 'biscuit book' — its house style of approachable explainer guides — covering AI, data science and machine learning, including how it measures success and where these methods have limits. It signals Dstl trying to make its AI work more legible to industry, partners and its own staff.
latest.sourceName: GOV.UK — AI, Data Science and Machine Learning: a Dstl biscuit book

latest.label: Asking industry where to focus
latest.plain: Dstl's AI programme ran a public survey asking companies in intelligence-analysis software and services about research priorities — in effect, checking that MOD-funded AI research is aimed at the right problems. A small but telling window into how Dstl steers its AI agenda with the market.
latest.sourceName: UK Parliament — written question: Dstl Artificial Intelligence

suppliers.name: Kainos
suppliers.role: prime — AI Agile Delivery Partner on Dstl's £7M experimentation contract
suppliers.claim: Kainos says it brings "specialist expertise and experience as a leader in the civil world into defence" with a "proven track record of bringing experimental AI to successful front-line operation" as Dstl's AI Agile Delivery Partner.
suppliers.sourceName: Kainos — Dstl announce Kainos as AI Agile Delivery Partner

suppliers.name: Faculty (Faculty Science Ltd)
suppliers.role: partner — AI specialist on the Dstl/DAIC-X experimentation work
suppliers.claim: Faculty says it "brought experts, end users and ethicists into the delivery cycle and solved the key problems of trust, assurance, safety and security that often stop AI crossing 'the valley of death'."

suppliers.name: Actica Consulting
suppliers.role: partner — defence delivery partner on the Dstl experimentation contract

suppliers.name: University of Exeter (DDRC consortium)
suppliers.role: partner — leads the Defence Data Research Centre (with Liverpool, Surrey, Digital Catapult)
suppliers.sourceName: GOV.UK — Dstl launches Defence Data Research Centre

suppliers.name: The Alan Turing Institute
suppliers.role: partner — co-runs the Defence Centre for AI Research (DCAR) with Dstl

sources.name: AI and data science: defence S&T capability (GOV.UK)

unknowns: Total AI spend and the value/duration of the Serapis framework are not consistently disclosed in primary sources, and fielded operational AI systems are largely classified — the public domain shows capability-building, research and contracts, not deployed tools. Exact start dates for the AI Lab brand and some hub milestones are approximate, anchored to the page/press dates rather than formal launch records.
